skill.name: julia-pro
skill.description: Master Julia 1.10+ with modern features, performance optimization, multiple dispatch, and production-ready practices. Expert in the Julia ecosystem including package management, scientific computing, and high-performance numerical code. Use PROACTIVELY for Julia development, optimization, or advanced Julia patterns.
skill.summary: Master Julia 1.10+ with modern features, performance optimization, multiple dispatch, and production...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command syntax, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown formatting and Julia domain terms. No evidence found of executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: The external command finding at line 27 is a Markdown inline-code reference to another resource, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are benign Julia documentation terms such as multiple dispatch, REST APIs, package registration, and metaprogramming.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are descriptive Markdown content and contain no executable code or instructions to run commands. The matched words are used in Julia programming context, not in cryptographic or reconnaissance operations.

content.value_statement: Julia projects can become slow, hard to type-check, or difficult to package. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through modern Julia design, testing, profiling, and deployment practices.
actual_capabilities: Design Julia type hierarchies and multiple dispatch APIs.
Improve type stability, memory allocation, and runtime performance.
Plan Julia package structure with PkgTemplates.jl and Documenter.jl.
Set up testing, coverage, benchmarking, and quality checks.
Advise on scientific computing, data science, web API, and GPU workflows.
Recommend Julia tooling such as JET.jl, Aqua.jl, Revise.jl, and BenchmarkTools.jl.
limitations: It provides guidance and generated examples, not guaranteed benchmark results.
It depends on the model having enough project context to make safe design choices.
It does not replace domain validation for numerical methods or scientific models.
It may need current package documentation for recently changed APIs.

use_cases.title: Optimize Numerical Code
use_cases.description: Find type instability, reduce allocations, and plan profiling steps for performance-sensitive Julia functions.

use_cases.title: Create a Julia Package
use_cases.description: Plan package structure, tests, documentation, formatting, and continuous integration for a new Julia library.

use_cases.title: Design Data and API Workflows
use_cases.description: Choose Julia packages and architecture for data pipelines, web APIs, machine learning, or visualization projects.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Julia Function
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Julia function for readability, type stability, and common performance issues. Explain the most important changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Julia package structure for this project. Include tests, documentation, formatting, CI, and recommended package tools.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Dispatch Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Julia API using multiple dispatch and parametric types. Preserve behavior and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a Production Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a profiling and optimization plan for this Julia workflow. Include BenchmarkTools, allocation analysis, type inference checks, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: Optimize a slow Julia simulation loop.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering type stability, allocation sources, profiling commands, benchmark design, and safer loop or data-structure changes.

output_examples.input: Create a package plan for a numerical methods library.
output_examples.output: A package layout with module boundaries, test strategy, documentation plan, quality checks, and release steps.

output_examples.input: Design a multiple dispatch hierarchy for model types.
output_examples.output: A dispatch-oriented API plan with abstract types, concrete implementations, method responsibilities, and type piracy warnings.

best_practices: Provide representative inputs, performance goals, and current bottlenecks.
Ask for tests and benchmarks alongside optimization suggestions.
Include Project.toml context when package compatibility matters.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad optimization without a workload or benchmark target.
Do not use generated APIs without reviewing type ownership and dispatch behavior.
Do not treat suggested numerical methods as validated scientific results.

faq.question: What Julia versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Julia 1.10 and newer, with emphasis on current tooling and production practices.

faq.question: Can it help with performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on type stability, allocations, profiling, benchmarking, and dispatch design.

faq.question: Does it create complete Julia packages?
faq.answer: It can plan and draft package structure, tests, documentation, and CI guidance.

faq.question: Can it work with scientific computing libraries?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common Julia tools for linear algebra, differential equations, optimization, statistics, and visualization.

faq.question: Does it run Julia code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is instructional text and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production systems?
faq.answer: It supports production planning, but users should run tests, benchmarks, and security reviews in their own environment.
